positions.label: Uranus eve on the Don flank

sources.citation: Niehorster, L. — German 6th Army order of battle, 19 Nov 1942
Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

de-h-ak-11.notes: Pilot coverage: Case Blue to the surrender.

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014
Niehorster, L. — German 6th Army order of battle, 19 Nov 1942

de-h-pz-div-24.notes: Converted from 1. Kavallerie-Division (Nov 1941). Reformed Mar 1943 — separate incarnation, not yet authored. Pilot: corps assignment held constant over the pilot window; positions derived from the corps sector (approximate).

su-front-southwestern.notes: Scaffold (no positions yet). Second formation; northern pincer of Uranus.

sources.citation: Glantz, D. & House, J. — Stalingrad trilogy (To the Gates of Stalingrad; Armageddon in Stalingrad; Endgame at Stalingrad), 2009–2014

su-rd-284.notes: Second formation, Siberian. Later 79th Guards. Pilot: city positions schematic (west-bank strip); the division held a narrow riverside sector inside the drawn pocket ring, so side checks report it as contested.
